title: Achillea × tzvelevii
intro: Achillea × tzvelevii, also known as Tzvelev's yarrow, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Achillea, characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small, white or yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Achillea × tzvelevii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Achillea × tzvelevii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flower extracts, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Achillea × tzvelevii is a nothospecific hybrid of the plant genus Achillea. It is believed to be a cross between two species of the genus. The hybrid is part of the Asteraceae family, which consists of various flowering plants. Achillea × tzvelevii is primarily found in areas with temperate climates. The classification of Achillea × tzvelevii is a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to change as new information is discovered.
uses: Achillea × tzvelevii is known to have potential medicinal properties, particularly in the realm of traditional herbal medicine.
